message: Can you believe it? That influencer photoshoot is blowing up! We're making waves all over social media.
Our watches and loafers are all over everyone's feeds
You're telling me? My inbox is packed with customer queries

Welcome to Discount Disaster the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Yeah I'm seeing it traffic's surging. But nothing we didn't anticipate for
Good. We've optimised the backend for this kind of spike
Let's just make sure that's all it is - legit traffic. Influencer campaigns can attract more than just customers
I'll keep eyes on the dashboard. We're running at nearly 80% capacity right now for all the user actvity our platform can handle
It's all good. People are scrambling to get their hands on our products. That viral post is gold
We're inching closer to max capacity. We're at 95% load now
I just refreshed the website and it's now taking ages to load
Is the website down? Or maybe it's just my machine
@daniel is there any clear indication on why we are reaching max capacity ?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Traffic is ramping up faster than we projected
I see that HTTP Status: 503 Service Unavailable we have this error on the site
:star: Reproduces the incident
We’re already brushing against upper frontend thresholds
Influencer traffic can be unpredictable
I’m noticing delayed responses even on simple endpoints
@daniel can we increase the max capasity?
Dashboards show elevated latency across frontend pods
@daniel what might cause this latency?
We are already at max capacity unfortunatly not an option
Perhaps a throttling
We’ve had a few production deployments today but nothing unusual on the surface
Yeah i see 6500 users in user activity
:star: Use metrics as evidence
Initial checks don’t show obvious failures in backend services
@shay  these Production Deployments what were they exactly?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YmMxM2RhNDA1YWY0NGZmY2E0NzJmN2M5YWJmOGRhOGM\nHere's the list of all the changes we have made
@shay could these Production Changes be cause the issue>
:star: Reviewed changes
:bulb: Who in the organisation can help you understand how customers are affected?
This level of attention is great but we need to make sure the platform keeps up
@bob can you tell us what the customers are experiencing?
We are getting a lot of calls from customers that they are unable to access the website
Payment AdService and Product Catalogue all appear stable
Few are reporting long loading times
and few are able to load platforms but unable to order
Here are the latest reports
Frontend is the only service showing signs of real distress
Looks like a global issue
Even so the behaviour doesn’t fully match a bad release
@shay  i seen that change CHG85387 you tested for only 5000 users
Deployment timestamps don’t line up cleanly with the start of the incident
That said we expected peaks around 4k requests per minute not 6.5k
@shay  is this the maximum that we can support?
5k was the upper limit we have configured it to
This exceeds what we validated in UAT
@daniel Can we make some sort of queing system? so that the customers can see that the site is working but just overloaded ?
There are active stress tests in UAT but they’re isolated environments
@shay  These active stress test plese stop those and lets see if that helps
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I can work on that but that will take time since we have nothing in place for that
Understood!
@daniel how much time will that take?
:star: Manage actions and timings
*Incident Number INC100095:* - Site is getting intermittedend disruption due to customers overload
I will try my best to get it done in next 30 mins
I have stopped all stress tests
Shay and I will check the logs and services
currently INC100095 is a p2
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Im seeing a lot of errors in the logs
Not sure what the root cause is but we are seeing high load on our Frontend Pods - this may be due to the ongoing discount or if it could be related to any tests we were running.
I see high number of rest-call errors like  http429 http503 and 504s

```
ERROR: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 429 200 - Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36 FrontEndApp
``` ```

```
Im also seeing this
@Shay can you elaborate
```\n[error]: Service unavailable cannot process request for /api/GetInfo (503).\n``` ```
Seeing frontend errors as well

```
[error] 12345#12345: *98765 limiting requests exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176 request: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 FrontEndApp
``` 
```[error] 12345#12345: *98766 limiting requests exceeded 10 requests per second for client 149.34.52.176 request: GET /api/GetInfo HTTP/1.1 FrontEndApp

``` ```
Do we know what the expected amount of traffic should be due to the on-going discount?
I think we need help from tanya or hamed on this
So these gateaway timeouts can that be a ddos atack?
:star: Form a working theory
@tanya Please join
@hamed Please join
:star: Escalating when required
abnormal patterns can hide inside expected spikes worth checking the traffic
@shay  Tell Tanya and hamed what you need
From a network standpoint this doesn’t resemble normal customer distribution
I was looking at Network traffic - But the dashboard took ages to load.
Here's a screenshot of Network traffic across each region.
@tanya  could this be a DDOS atacK
Wow Spain must really like our products
hmm... @maya what do you think?
@maya  can you join please
@tanya We’re seeing a high volume of HTTP 429s followed by 503s as load increases. Pod resource usage is pegged — autoscaling isn’t keeping up
@maya this traffic from spain could that be a DDOS attac ?
Campaigns tend to attract noise as well as customers. Worth keeping that in mind
I will start investigation!
Adding to that — request patterns look highly repetitive
The same endpoint is being hit over and over with minimal variance
@daniel  that would point to a DDOS attack
:star: Qualify the problem
Traffic concentration is unusually skewed
@maya  what is your verdict?
Let's see what maya diggs up
Hey @arek.jadwiszczak-thomsonreuters.com after reviewing the logs I strongly suspect that this is a security-related issue.
The abnormally high traffic with http 429 503 and 504 errors may be due to a possible DDoS/DoS attack
I'll need to continue investigating further but it definitely looks like something is targeting our system
Looks like we have a security threat - We should be extra careful with this incident
That’s not typical even for viral events
Do we know what's the financial impact of the issue? How much are we losing every minute?
@arek.jadwiszczak-thomsonreuters.com Should we inform the 'Compliance Team' about this issue?
The Compliance Team makes sure we follow laws and rules like reporting the incident to the right people and keeping proper records. It helps us avoid legal trouble and protects our reputation.
@maya so how much time you need for additional investigation?
I recommend reading this document to undergo the mitigation steps\nhttps://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/769163265/DoS+V+DDoS+Attack+Understanding+the+Difference
@bob can you in the meantime work on what we are going to tell our customers - why we had these outages? and do not mention anything about security breaches or attacks
:star: Know your team and their roles
Understood I will reach out to the compliance team
@maya  do we have a WAF deployeD ?
Leave Compliance with me - I will talk to them
Indeed we do!
This appears to be a DoS attack not distributed — traffic originates from one IP
@maya  can you block this IP that it is commnig from?
Already working on this!
FYI compliance should be aware but disclosure isn’t automatic
@bob  how many customers do we have in Spain? is it worth considering to do Geo-blocking for spain?
:bulb: keep the business informed regularly
to contain the issue>
about 9% of sales are originating from spain
The IP address has now been blocked
Can you confirm @arek.jadwiszczak-thomsonreuters.com?
Lucky this was DoS (Denial of Service ) from a single IP - What if this attack happens from multiple IP addresses (DDoS)?
Issue seem to be a DDOS attack from Spain security team allready working on it
We should start working on the pending WAF rule updates for the frontend
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
@arek.jadwiszczak-thomsonreuters.com Should we let the customers know about the attack?
Compliance team has been informed
@bob no we should not find some onther feasable excuse
@tinus chacking
Because this is a single-source attack blocking the IP should immediately relieve pressure and we should be fine by not Geoblocking the Spain
@tinus Site is back up
@tinus can you check as well?
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Agree we are not to do it until we discuss with compliance
This could have been much worse if it were distributed
Issue seem to have been resolved after blockage of the attacking IP adress business validations in progress
I'm able to place an order! Are we good here?
@tinus I believe so
Great work we managed to save the sale
I knew we had it in us
Now i can continue shopping and get some nice discounts
I was able to place an order as well
Absolutely Shay! But i hope not during work hours
Of course Daniel...... Sorry team i got to go close a unrelated tab now
Anyways well done team
You all did an amazing job
Issue is confirmed to be solved site is back up and orders can be placed
